Ibuprofen-danger.description: Both medications inhibit COX enzymes and platelet aggregation
Ibuprofen-danger.mechanism: AVOID this combination if possible. If unavoidable under doctor supervision
Ibuprofen-danger.alternatives: Use acetaminophen (Tylenol) for pain relief instead of ibuprofen
Ibuprofen-danger.monitoring_advice: Watch for black/tarry stools, coffee ground vomit, easy bruising

Warfarin-danger.description: NSAIDs increase bleeding risk and can cause gastric ulcers
Warfarin-danger.mechanism: AVOID this combination. Use acetaminophen for pain instead.
Warfarin-danger.spacing_effectiveness: Acetaminophen is safer for pain relief
Warfarin-danger.alternatives: Monitor for bleeding; consider PPI for stomach protection

Lisinopril-warning.mechanism: Monitor blood sugar levels closely when starting this combination.
Lisinopril-warning.spacing_effectiveness: Alternative BP meds may have less effect on glucose
Lisinopril-warning.alternatives: Check blood sugar more frequently, especially after meals

Warfarin-warning.description: Phenytoin induces CYP enzymes affecting warfarin metabolism
Warfarin-warning.mechanism: Monitor INR closely when starting/stopping phenytoin.
Warfarin-warning.spacing_effectiveness: Alternative anticonvulsants may have less interaction
Warfarin-warning.alternatives: Check INR weekly when changing doses

Amiodarone-danger.description: Amiodarone inhibits CYP3A4 enzyme increasing statin levels
Amiodarone-danger.mechanism: AVOID this combination. Use alternative statin (pravastatin/rosuvastatin).
Amiodarone-danger.spacing_effectiveness: Pravastatin or rosuvastatin are safer alternatives
Amiodarone-danger.alternatives: Monitor for muscle pain, weakness; check CK levels

Nitroglycerin-danger.description: Both are vasodilators causing synergistic blood pressure drop
Nitroglycerin-danger.mechanism: ABSOLUTE CONTRAINDICATION - do not use within 24-48 hours.
Nitroglycerin-danger.spacing_effectiveness: For ED: tadalafil also contraindicated; discuss non-nitrate options
Nitroglycerin-danger.alternatives: Seek emergency care if chest pain occurs after taking

Calcium-warning.description: Calcium binds to levothyroxine in the gut
Calcium-warning.mechanism: Separate doses by at least 4 hours.
Calcium-warning.spacing_effectiveness: Take calcium at bedtime if levothyroxine is morning
Calcium-warning.alternatives: Check TSH levels 6-8 weeks after changing schedule

Furosemide-warning.mechanism: Monitor potassium levels and digoxin levels.
Furosemide-warning.alternatives: Check potassium and digoxin levels regularly

Omeprazole-warning.description: Omeprazole inhibits CYP2C19 needed to activate clopidogrel
Omeprazole-warning.mechanism: Consider alternative PPI (pantoprazole) or H2 blocker.
Omeprazole-warning.spacing_effectiveness: Pantoprazole, famotidine, or ranitidine have less interaction
Omeprazole-warning.alternatives: Monitor for cardiovascular events; consider platelet function testing

NSAIDs-danger.description: NSAIDs reduce renal clearance of lithium
NSAIDs-danger.mechanism: AVOID NSAIDs if on lithium. Use acetaminophen for pain.
NSAIDs-danger.spacing_effectiveness: Acetaminophen is safe; consider alternative mood stabilizers
NSAIDs-danger.alternatives: Monitor lithium levels closely; check renal function

Penicillin-warning.description: Penicillin reduces renal excretion of methotrexate
Penicillin-warning.mechanism: Monitor methotrexate levels; adjust dose if needed.
Penicillin-warning.spacing_effectiveness: Alternative antibiotics may have less interaction
Penicillin-warning.alternatives: Check methotrexate levels; monitor for toxicity signs

Metoprolol-danger.description: Both medications slow AV node conduction
Metoprolol-danger.mechanism: AVOID this combination. Consider alternative calcium channel blocker.
Metoprolol-danger.spacing_effectiveness: Amlodipine or nifedipine have less heart rate effect
Metoprolol-danger.alternatives: Monitor heart rate and ECG; watch for dizziness/fainting

Tizanidine-danger.description: Ciprofloxacin inhibits CYP1A2 increasing tizanidine levels
Tizanidine-danger.mechanism: AVOID this combination. If necessary reduce tizanidine dose by 75%.
Tizanidine-danger.alternatives: Monitor BP and sedation levels closely

Oral Contraceptives-warning.description: Rifampin induces CYP enzymes increasing hormone metabolism
Oral Contraceptives-warning.spacing_effectiveness: IUD, barrier methods, or progestin-only options
Oral Contraceptives-warning.alternatives: Watch for breakthrough bleeding; consider pregnancy test

Ciprofloxacin-warning.description: Ciprofloxacin inhibits CYP1A2 reducing theophylline clearance
Ciprofloxacin-warning.mechanism: Reduce theophylline dose by 30-50% and monitor levels.
Ciprofloxacin-warning.alternatives: Monitor theophylline levels; watch for nausea/vomiting

Cranberry Juice-warning.description: Unknown mechanism - possibly inhibition of CYP enzymes
Cranberry Juice-warning.mechanism: Limit cranberry juice intake; monitor INR more frequently.
Cranberry Juice-warning.spacing_effectiveness: Avoid cranberry juice; other juices are safe
Cranberry Juice-warning.alternatives: Check INR within 1 week of consuming cranberry

Grapefruit Juice-warning.interaction_type: Increased statin levels and muscle risk
Grapefruit Juice-warning.description: Grapefruit inhibits CYP3A4 in gut wall
Grapefruit Juice-warning.mechanism: AVOID grapefruit juice; take with water instead.
Grapefruit Juice-warning.spacing_effectiveness: Orange juice is safe; other citrus fruits are fine
Grapefruit Juice-warning.alternatives: Monitor for muscle pain; consider switching statin

MAOI-danger.spacing_effectiveness: Use different antidepressant class; allow proper washout
MAOI-danger.alternatives: Watch for agitation, confusion, fever, muscle rigidity

Potassium Supplement-warning.mechanism: Monitor potassium levels; avoid potassium-containing salt substitutes.
Potassium Supplement-warning.spacing_effectiveness: Use alternative BP meds if recurrent hyperkalemia
Potassium Supplement-warning.alternatives: Check potassium levels regularly; watch for weakness
